summary: Scheduled recurring jobs (crontab) over the same part-owned Postgres queue. A cron schedule on the worker config enqueues recurring jobs on their pattern; missed runs are backfilled. Wraps graphile-worker's crontab.

exports: WorkerConfig.cron?: CronSchedule  (CronSchedule = Array<{ task: string; pattern: string; payload?: object; identifier?: string; backfillSeconds?: number }>)
runWorker(config: WorkerConfig): Promise<RunningWorker>  — executes the cron schedule (server shape)
drainOnce(config: WorkerConfig): Promise<void>  — processes due jobs (serverless shape; the platform cron is the trigger)

invariants: A cron schedule registers recurring jobs that the long-running worker executes on their pattern; a missed run within the configured backfill window is enqueued on startup (verified via backfill)
An invalid cron pattern is rejected at config time with JobsError('invalid_input') — no worker starts and no job is scheduled
notes: Same part as jobs.queue (one part, two capabilities — graphile-worker does both). jobs.cron@1 is the scheduling layer over jobs.queue@1's durable queue: a `cron` array on the worker config maps to graphile-worker's crontab. In the SERVER shape (runWorker) graphile-worker's scheduler enqueues recurring jobs and backfills missed runs; in the SERVERLESS shape the platform's cron (e.g. a Vercel/Cloud cron hitting a route) is the trigger that calls drainOnce or enqueue (a long-running cron scheduler is a daemon feature by nature — docs/05 §1, the honest exception). Conformance verifies cron scheduling via backfill against real graphile-worker + Postgres, and the config-time rejection of an invalid pattern DB-free. Suite lives with the implementing part until a second provider exists (docs/02 §3-4).
